channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Netanyahu is testifying in his corruption trial. He has been dogged by scandal for years
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu took the witness stand for the first time in his long-running trial on corruption charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least 8 killed in building collapse in Cairo
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — An apartment building collapsed on Tuesday in Egypt’s capital, killing at least eight people, authorities said. The Health Ministry said in a statement that the collapse of the six-story building in Cairo’s western neighborhood of Waili also injured three people, who were hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbia's president says he won't flee his country like Syria's Assad did despite growing protests
item.description: Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić says he isn't like ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad and has no intention of fleeing the country despite spreading protests against his increasingly autocratic rule.
item.dc:creator: Dušan Stojanović, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France flies out 2 Mirage fighter jets from Chad to signal beginning of military withdrawal
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France flew out the two Mirage fighter jets it had stationed in Chad on Tuesday, signaling the beginning of the withdrawal of its military forces from the former French colony in Central Africa that last month broke off its defense cooperation agreement with Paris.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese military makes massive deployment around Taiwan to send 'very simple' message
item.description: Taiwan is raising concerns about a massive deployment of Chinese naval ships and military planes in the past 24 hours, saying the build-up could eventually lead to war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK and Germany sign deal against people smugglers as Europe struggles to halt Channel crossings
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. and Germany pledged Tuesday to share intelligence and expertise against the people-smuggling gangs that send migrants across the English Channel in small boats, the latest effort by European countries to stop the dangerous journeys.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police in Kenya hurl tear gas at protesters angry about gender-based violence
item.description: Police in Kenya’s capital have hurled tear gas canisters at hundreds of protesters angry about gender-based violence and femicide or the killing of women.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi And Brian Inganga, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taiwan's Defense Ministry says a large Chinese military force has been detected near island
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's secretive military appears to be up to something around Taiwan, but it's unclear whether it's a formal military drill.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trudeau says Americans are realizing Trump's tariffs on Canada would make life a lot more expensive
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Monday that Americans “are beginning to wake up to the real reality that tariffs on everything from Canada would make life a lot more expensive" and said he will retaliate if Donald Trump goes ahead with them.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greyhound racing is increasingly rare worldwide. New Zealand now plans to outlaw the practice
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — New Zealand plans to outlaw greyhound racing because too many dogs are hurt or killed, the government said Tuesday, spelling an end to the practice in one of the few countries where it still operates.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Netanyahu testifies in his corruption trial as warplanes hit sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli warplanes have pounded military sites in Syria after Israeli troops seized a border buffer zone following the collapse of President Bashar Assad’s rule.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US defense secretary in Japan to support alliance as Osprey aircraft safety causes concern
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin has met with officials in Japan to reaffirm the importance of their alliance and Washington's commitment to regional security as threats rise from China and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Illia Novikov And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on Chinese gold mining threatening endangered protected site in Congo
item.description: OKAPI WILDLIFE RESERVE, Congo (AP) — For eight years, a Chinese mining company has been vastly expanding inside an endangered World Heritage Site, accused by locals and conservationists of decimating the environment.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes hit Gaza and also target suspected weapons sites in Syria
item.description: Israeli strikes killed at least six people in the central Gaza Strip, Palestinian medical officials said Monday, while also hitting suspected chemical and long-range weapons sites in Syria to keep them from rebels who seized Damascus. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ghana's ex-leader is declared the presidential election winner and pledges 'reset' in a bad economy
item.description: ACCRA, Ghana (AP) — Ghana’s former leader John Dramani Mahama was declared the winner of the presidential election on Monday and pledged a “a life of limitless opportunity” for citizens after voters vented their anger over the country’s worst cost-of-living crisis in a generation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCIS KOKUTSE and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon chief: ‘Everybody expected’ stiffer resistance from Assad in Syria
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin said the international community was expecting Syrian President Bashar Assad to have a stronger response to the rebels that took control of Damascus over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Incoming Trump envoy presses Syrian rebels to release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming hostage affairs envoy Adam Boehler pressed the rebels in Syria to release the American hostages in the country as the region grapples with more instability.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian government still functioning: Prime minister
item.description: Syria’s prime minister said the country’s government is still functioning and most Cabinet ministers are still working, even after rebels entered Damascus and President Bashar Assad fled the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Assad granted asylum in Russia, arrives in Moscow
item.description: Syrian President Bashar Assad and his family have been granted asylum in Russia and arrived in Moscow on Sunday after the Syrian government fell.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian dictator Bashar Assad flees into exile as Islamist rebels conquer country
item.description: A lightning Syrian Islamist offensive over just days contributed to the demise of the over 50-year reign of the Assad Baathist regime in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach gates of Damascus, threatening decades-long Assad regime
item.description: Syrian insurgents have reached the gates of the nation's capital, shortly after Bashar al-Assad's government abandoned the key city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alternative healer gets 10 years in UK prison for death of woman at slap therapy workshop
item.description: Hongchi Xiao, a 61-year-old alternative healer who used 'slapping therapy' to treat maladies, has been sentenced to 10 years in prison for the death of a 71-year-old woman.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Metal detectorist uncovers fragment of Viking sword in the Netherlands
item.description: A 10th-century Viking sword fragment was discovered by a metal detectorist scouring a field in the Netherlands. Further research is being done to find out more about the ancient item.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alleged Chinese cyberattack: FBI urges encrypted apps
item.description: Salt Typhoon, a Chinese government-linked hacking group, is one of the largest intelligence breaches in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation
